mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market demand, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in PAYEMS > 150k periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic activity, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO/HOUST growth periods are negative or pairwise correlation between proxies drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) signals a shift in demand toward services over goods, creating positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle transitions.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in RSAFS/HOUST divergent periods are negative or interaction effect size < 0.15 in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 250 signals central bank policy inaction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as real rates decline.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPIAUCSL > 250 periods are positive or inflation-unemployment trade-off breaks down (Phillips curve R² < 0.3).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) combined with weak job growth (PAYEMS < 100k) signals wage-price pressures, creating negative S&P 500 returns during stagflation risks.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE/PAYEMS divergent periods are positive or wage growth fails to correlate with inflation (Pearson < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) during rising housing starts (HOUST > 1100) indicates resilience in consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1/HOUST divergent periods are negative or GDP growth fails to predict equity returns (R² < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.5 signals manufacturing weakness, creating negative S&P 500 returns during supply-side shocks.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO decline periods are positive or industrial output fails to correlate with equity volatility (Spearman < 0.15).
